name: Vía Verde de las Malas Tardes and Laguna del Prado Loop
name_original: Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular desde Ciudad Real
description: Explore the Campo de Calatrava on this moderate 22.8-mile (36.7 km) touring cycling route, featuring Laguna del Prado and historic sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Campo De Calatrava traverse a landscape shaped by its unique volcanic origins, featuring over 300 volcanic structures including maars and pyroclastic cones. The region presents a diverse natural tapestry of vast steppe lands, plains, and Mediterranean mountains, with the Guadiana River providing vital waterways. Cyclists can expect undulating roads and climbs, especially when approaching historical sites like medieval castles, and routes often lead across expansive fields of cereals, vines, and olive trees. Best touring cycling routes around Campo De Calatrava The most popular touring cycling route is Pantano del Vicario from Ciudad Real — circular via the Puente de Hierro, a 27.4 miles (44.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Vicario Reservoir and passes the historic Iron Bridge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde de las Malas Tardes and Laguna del Prado Loop, a moderate 22.8 miles (36.7 km) path. This trail follows a greenway and explores the natural beauty around Laguna del Prado. Local touring cyclists also love the Poblete from Ciudad Real — circular via the Poblete Greenway, a 15.9 miles (25.5 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Campo De Calatrava is defined by its volcanic landscapes, historical castles, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging routes with elevation gain. The routes in Campo De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campo De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Campo De Calatrava?
A: Campo De Calatrava offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 240 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 54 easy, 155 moderate, and 39 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on touring cycling routes in Campo De Calatrava?
A: The region's unique volcanic origins mean you'll cycle through landscapes featuring over 300 volcanic structures, including maars (water-filled craters) and pyroclastic cones. Expect a mix of vast steppe lands, plains, and Mediterranean mountains. While many areas are predominantly flat, routes often include undulating roads and climbs, especially when approaching historical sites. You'll also traverse expansive fields of cereals, vines, and olive trees.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Campo De Calatrava has 54 easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052765066/las-casas-circular-from-ciudad-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Casas — circular from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 21.5 km (13.4 miles) loop with minimal elevation gain, offering a pleasant ride through the local scenery.

Q: What historical landmarks and natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in both history and natural beauty. You can explore medieval castles like the imposing Castillo de Calatrava la Nueva, and visit historic towns such as Almagro with its well-preserved 17th-century theater. Natural features include unique maar lakes like the Hoya de Cervera Lagoon and De La Posadilla Lagoon, as well as the vital Guadiana River. Specific routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056711125/pantano-del-vicario-from-ciudad-real-circular-via-the-puente-de-hierro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano del Vicario from Ciudad Real — circular via the Puente de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; pass by the historic Iron Bridge and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that explore the volcanic features of the region?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its extensive volcanic field. For a route specifically designed to showcase these features, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058398306/calatrava-volcanoes-loop-from-argamasilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatrava Volcanoes Loop from Argamasilla&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 47.7 km (29.6 miles) route offers significant elevation gain and takes you through the heart of the volcanic landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Campo De Calatrava?
A: The most pleasant times for touring cycling in Campo De Calatrava are spring and autumn. During these seasons, temperatures are more moderate, making for comfortable rides. Summers can be intensely hot, and winters potentially cold, so planning your trip for spring or autumn is recommended.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes or bikepacking options?
A: Yes, the region is suitable for multi-day cycling adventures. While specific bikepacking routes are not detailed here, the overall network includes longer options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052134910/vicario-reservoir-loop-from-ciudad-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Reservoir Loop from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 76.7 km (47.7 miles) route, offering a longer day trip or a segment of a multi-day tour.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Campo De Calatrava?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Campo De Calatrava are circular, making them convenient for day trips. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052753879/via-verde-de-las-malas-tardes-and-laguna-del-prado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de las Malas Tardes and Laguna del Prado Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.7 km (22.8 miles) path, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052149908/poblete-from-ciudad-real-circular-via-the-poblete-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblete from Ciudad Real — circular via the Poblete Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.5 km (15.9 miles) trail.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Campo De Calatrava?
A: The touring cycling routes in Campo De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from flat greenways to challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore both the unique volcanic landscapes and rich historical sites like castles and ancient towns.

Q: Are there any routes that follow greenways or dedicated cycling paths?
A: Yes, the region features greenways that provide pleasant and often flatter cycling experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052753879/via-verde-de-las-malas-tardes-and-laguna-del-prado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de las Malas Tardes and Laguna del Prado Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, following a greenway and exploring the natural beauty around Laguna del Prado. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052149908/poblete-from-ciudad-real-circular-via-the-poblete-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblete from Ciudad Real — circular via the Poblete Greenway&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, Campo De Calatrava offers routes with notable elevation gain, particularly those venturing into the more mountainous or volcanic areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052704692/campo-de-calatrava-circular-from-ciudad-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava — circular from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 66.1 km (41.1 miles) route with over 500 meters (1,640 feet) of ascent, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: Are there routes that pass by reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the region's water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056711125/pantano-del-vicario-from-ciudad-real-circular-via-the-puente-de-hierro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano del Vicario from Ciudad Real — circular via the Puente de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Vicario Reservoir. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058505797/presa-de-jabalon-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; takes you around the Jabalón Dam, providing scenic waterside cycling.
tips: Chozo (or small hut) refuge of branches or stone that was built both in the open in mountainous areas and in the groves, vacant lots or meadows of the fields, and that was used by shepherds and farmers to spend the night with the flock or to protect themselves from the inclement weather, during peasant work.

The parish church of San Juan Bautista is a temple from the 18th century, with a Latin cross and a single nave. As annexes it has a tower, attached to the foot of the church and sacristy, attached to the right side of the temple and to one of the arms of the transept. The roof is made of curved tiles, and the structure is made of masonry on the walls, ashlar on the buttresses and brick on the arches.

Of the interior it is necessary to emphasize the half orange dome on pendentives that finish off the transept. The structure is supported by four stone columns, two of them uncovered and two plastered.

It is located next to the urban area of Pozuelo de Calatrava. It is popularly known as the unexpected. If you pass by, it is a unique opportunity to enjoy the flora and fauna of the saltiest lagoon in the area.
Miguelturra Town Hall bullring, with a good parking area.
Tribute to the working women of Barrio Oriente. Miguelturra.
The Greenway is about 500 meters of good and flat road.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular from Ciudad Real route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting from Ciudad Real, you can typically find parking within the city itself, especially near the starting point of the trail. Alternatively, you could park in Pozuelo de Calatrava if you prefer to begin your ride from there, though Ciudad Real offers more options.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is categorized as moderate, covering 36.7 kilometers with a modest elevation gain of about 90 meters. While it requires good fitness, it's also described as suitable for all skill levels due to its largely paved surfaces. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should find it manageable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect largely paved surfaces throughout the 36.7-kilometer route. The elevation changes are gentle, indicating a generally flat or gently rolling profile, making for a smooth cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle in Castilla-La Mancha, where this route is located, are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular from Ciudad Real route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this particular cycling route, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when cycling near agricultural areas or through towns. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Campo de Calatrava area?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route or to access the general Campo de Calatrava area. It's freely accessible for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: What interesting sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include a traditional Chozo, the historic Church of San Juan Bautista, and the serene Laguna del Prado. You'll also pass by a Bullring and a Monument, offering glimpses into the local culture and landscape.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it includes a section of the Vía Verde de las Malas Tardes. You'll also find it crosses parts of the Camino de la Mata, Camino del Terrero, and the Camino de Carrión de Calatrava a Almagro.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through Pozuelo de Calatrava and starts/ends in Ciudad Real, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in these towns where you can stop for refreshments, snacks, or a meal. It's always a good idea to carry some water and snacks, especially between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle ride?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle ride, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and a basic bike repair kit. Comfortable cycling attire and a helmet are essential. Given the rural sections, a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies is also wise.

faqs.question: Is the route well-signed?
faqs.answer: While the route largely follows established roads and paths, it's always recommended to use a reliable navigation tool, such as the komoot app, to ensure you stay on track. This will help you confidently navigate the circular path and enjoy the scenery without worrying about getting lost.
